methods.description: This method involves retrieving amino acid sequences, performing multiple sequence alignments, constructing a phylogenetic tree, and generating a sequence logo to analyze the evolutionary relationships and sequence conservation of OsPPR939 and its homologs.

steps.description: The full-length amino acid sequences of OsPPR939 and its 15 homologs were retrieved by BLASTP analysis (http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/Blast.cgi).
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve amino acid sequences using BLASTP
outputs: amino acid sequences of OsPPR939 and homologs

steps.description: Multiple protein sequence alignments were performed using Clustal X.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple sequence alignment with Clustal X

steps.description: A phylogenetic tree was constructed using MEGA v.6.0 based on the neighbor-joining method with the following parameters: p-distance model, pairwise deletion, and bootstrap (1000 replicates; random seed).
steps.searchable_summary: construct phylogenetic tree using MEGA v.6.0

steps.description: The 30 N-terminal amino acids of OsPPR939 and its Clustal X-aligned counterparts from the 15 homologs were used to generate a sequence logo using WebLogo.
steps.searchable_summary: generate sequence logo with WebLogo

methods.description: This method describes the extraction of total RNA from rice tissues, synthesis of cDNA, and subsequent RT-PCR and qRT-PCR analysis to study gene expression.

steps.description: Total RNA was isolated from rice tissues using RNAiso Plus reagent (TaKaRa).
steps.searchable_summary: isolate total RNA from rice tissues

steps.description: The quality and quantity of total RNA were analyzed using a NanoDrop 1000 spectrophotometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze RNA quality and quantity with NanoDrop

steps.description: Approximately 2 µg of RNA per sample was used to synthesize cDNA with a PrimeScript RT reagent Kit with gDNA eraser (TaKaRa).
steps.searchable_summary: synthesize cDNA from RNA

steps.description: qRT-PCR was performed on a LightCycler 96 using TaKaRa SYBR Premix Ex Taq II with a standard two-step protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: perform qRT-PCR analysis

methods.description: This method involves the functional complementation of mutant plants using a genomic DNA fragment of OsPPR939 to restore the wild type phenotype.

steps.description: Wild type (Nipponbare) genomic DNA was used as a template to amplify a 7191-bp genomic DNA fragment of OsPPR939.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify genomic DNA fragment of OsPPR939

steps.description: The amplified fragment was subcloned into the pCAMBIA1300 vector by In-Fusion cloning.
steps.searchable_summary: subclone DNA fragment into pCAMBIA1300 vector

steps.description: The resulting plasmid was transformed into Agrobacterium tumefaciens EHA105 for plant transformation.
steps.searchable_summary: transform plasmid into Agrobacterium for plant transformation

steps.description: Transformed plants were selected on hygromycin, and independent transgenic lines were identified by PCR analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: select and validate transgenic plants

methods.description: This method involves the phenotypic characterization of osppr939 mutants to assess growth, development, and fertility compared to wild type plants.

steps.description: osppr939-4 and osppr939-5 mutants were selected for phenotypic analysis based on their similar phenotypes.
steps.searchable_summary: select osppr939 mutants for phenotypic analysis

steps.description: Phenotypic traits such as growth, plant height, panicle length, and pollen fertility were assessed.
steps.searchable_summary: assess phenotypic traits of mutants

steps.description: Pollen grains were stained with iodine potassium iodide (I2-KI) to assess fertility.
steps.searchable_summary: stain pollen grains to assess fertility

steps.description: Scanning electron microscopy was used to confirm pollen phenotype.
steps.searchable_summary: use SEM to confirm pollen phenotype

methods.description: This method explores the evolutionary relationships of OsPPR939 by performing a BLASTP search and constructing a phylogenetic tree.

steps.description: A BLASTP search of the NCBI database was performed using the full-length OsPPR939 sequence as a query.
steps.searchable_summary: perform BLASTP search for OsPPR939 homologs

steps.description: Phylogenetic analysis of the 15 homologs retrieved from 15 different species was performed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform phylogenetic analysis of homologs

methods.description: This method examines the expression pattern of OsPPR939 using qRT-PCR in various tissues of wild type plants.

steps.description: Total RNA was extracted from vegetative and reproductive organs of wild type plants.
steps.searchable_summary: extract total RNA from plant tissues

steps.description: qRT-PCR was performed to examine the expression pattern of OsPPR939.
steps.searchable_summary: perform qRT-PCR for expression analysis
